title: excoecaria guineensis
intro: Excoecaria guineensis, also known as the poison tree or bushman poison, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its dark green, elliptical leaves and small, yellowish flowers. The plant contains toxic compounds, including phorbol esters and a toxic alkaloid, which have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. Excoecaria guineensis has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, often mixed with other herbs, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and gastrointestinal disorders.
